heroHeadline: TRUMP: NATO 'ABSOLUTELY USELESS' — 'STAY AWAY'; RUTTE: 'UNFOUNDED SPECULATION'; STOLTENBERG: 'NOT WRITTEN IN STONE'
heroSubtitle: Following the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz (April 17), President Trump escalated to his sharpest rebuff yet — calling NATO 'absolutely useless' and posting 'STAY AWAY' on Truth Social (April 18) when the alliance inquired whether the US needed post-ceasefire help. NATO Secretary General Rutte pushed back in a Welt am Sonntag interview (April 18), calling withdrawal fears 'unfounded speculation' and insisting the US nuclear umbrella 'remains the absolute guarantor of security in Europe.' Former Secretary General Stoltenberg had warned April 15 that 'it is not a natural law that NATO will exist for the next ten years.' Rutte also stated April 19 that Ukraine's NATO membership cannot be resolved 'in the short term,' naming the US, Germany, Slovakia, and Hungary as blockers. Europe's 40+ nation autonomous Hormuz coalition stands by. Hungary's PM-designate Magyar continues preparations to rebuild Budapest's NATO alignment ahead of the Helsingborg Foreign Ministers summit (May 21–22) and Ankara Summit (July 7–8).
footerNote: Data sourced from NATO HQ, White House, US Department of Defense, Reuters, Bloomberg, Wall Street Journal, The Economist, The Telegraph, TIME, Politico, German Federal Foreign Office, Axios, NPR, Washington Post, Al Jazeera, EU News, Swedish MFA, France 24, Euronews, CNN, PBS NewsHour, Gulf News, US Senate Foreign Relations Committee, GB News, Welt am Sonntag, Xinhua
